item.title: Clinical Trial: Immunovant's FcRn Drug (Phase 3 Thyroid Eye Disease)
item.description:
• Summary: This theme reports on clinical trial results for Immunovant's FcRn inhibitor drug in Thyroid Eye Disease (TED). The article states the drug failed in a pair of Phase 3 trials, representing a significant clinical setback for this investigational therapy in treating this autoimmune condition affecting the eyes and surrounding tissues.
  • MoAs: FcRn (neonatal Fc receptor) inhibitor. This mechanism of action works by blocking the FcRn receptor, which is responsible for recycling immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibodies, thereby accelerating the degradation of pathogenic autoantibodies that drive autoimmune diseases like Thyroid Eye Disease.
  • Sources: Endpoints News
